mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity cash flows, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hike periods with Rank IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and elevated consumer price indices indicate demand destruction under inflationary pressure, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of observations or interaction term Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, generating positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes fail to predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly translates to stronger corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation crosses above 290.0 index points, it forces central banks to accelerate rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and causing negative returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm labor market strength, supporting consumer spending and driving positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive returns with Rank IC > 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Falling housing starts signal reduced construction activity and lower consumer leverage, generating negative equity returns over 5 trading days during high-rate environments.
failure_condition: HOUST declines produce positive 5-day returns in > 65% of observations during tightening regimes.
